result:
Build is clean (no errors). Verified `errors.lua` now progresses further (past line 345's `print'+'`) — original bug is fixed.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:2587` (`cg_self`) — the OP_SELF codegen always emitted the method-name constant index as the C operand with `k=1`, even when the constant index exceeded the 8-bit `MAXINDEXRK` limit (255). The index got truncated to its low 8 bits, so `t:bbb()` after 1000+ constants reported the wrong method name (e.g. `'x233'` instead of `'bbb'`).

**Fix:** When `k_idx > MAXINDEXRK`, demote the key to `ExprKind::K` and discharge it into a register via `cg_exp_to_any_reg`, then emit OP_SELF with `C=reg, k=0`. Mirrors `codeABRK`/`exp2RK` from `lcode.c`.

**New state:** Reproducer (`t:bbb()` after 1000 globals) now reports `(method 'bbb')` correctly. `errors.lua` advances past the prior failure point — `>>> testC not active <<<` print at line 94 plus `+` print at line 345 now appear, with the next assertion failure being a separate bug for the next loop iteration.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/test_check.lua << 'EOF'
local function doit (s)
  local f, msg = load(s)
  if not f then return msg end
  local cond, msg = pcall(f)
  return (not cond) and msg
end

local function checksyntax (prog, extra, token, line)
  local msg = doit(prog)
  print("msg =", msg)
  if not string.find(token, "^<%a") and not string.find(token, "^char%(")
    then token = "'"..token.."'" end
  token = string.gsub(token, "(%p)", "%%%1")
  local pt = string.format([[^%%[string ".*"%%]:%d: .- near %s$]],
                           line, token)
  print("pt =", pt)
  local pos = string.find(msg, pt)
  print("pos =", pos)
  assert(pos)
end

checksyntax([[
  local a = {4

]], "'}' expected (to close '{' at line 1)", "<eof>", 3)

print("done")
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs /tmp/test_check.lua 2>&1 | tail -25

awk 'NR==40 {print "  print(\"PROBE msg=\", msg); print(\"PROBE pt=\", pt); print(\"PROBE find=\", string.find(msg, pt));" } {print}' reference/lua-c/testes/errors.lua > /tmp/errors_probe.lua && head -45 /tmp/errors_probe.lua | tail -15
